source: Etherscan getsourcecode (chainid=1, all verified non-proxy) + ProjectOpenSea/seaport-deployments
note: NFT-marketplace settlement engine. Onboarding scope = Seaport 1.6 core (cover); older versions + SeaDrop deferred with measured usage. Conduit/ConduitController excluded (approval operator / admin — setApprovalForAll already covered by standard/erc721). See crates/integration-tests/logs/seaport/P0_RESEARCH.md.

contracts.name: Seaport 1.6 (core settlement)
contracts.reason: primary; fulfill*/match*/cancel/incrementCounter calldata (Flow 1) + EIP-712 verifyingContract for signed OrderComponents (Flow 2). 95.9% of Seaport calls are top-level direct.

contracts.name: Seaport 1.5 (core settlement, prior version)
contracts.reason: DEFER (measured, user-facing but low/no usage — tracked in evidence Scope Classification): distinct verifyingContract; measured ~73 tx/14d (negligible). Same surface as 1.6 at a different address; orders bind to a specific version. Fail-closed to Unknown (not misrouted). Promote only if usage rises.

contracts.name: Seaport 1.4 (core settlement, prior version)
contracts.reason: DEFER (measured, user-facing but low/no usage — tracked in evidence Scope Classification): measured 0 tx/14d. Superseded by 1.5/1.6.

contracts.name: Conduit (OpenSea canonical, approval operator)
contracts.reason: approval operator, not a fulfill/order target. setApprovalForAll(operator=Conduit) is already covered by standard/erc721 (token::nft_set_approval_for_all). Recognized as the known-good operator inside order/approval legibility, not a separate covered surface.

contracts.name: ConduitController (conduit factory / channel admin)
contracts.reason: infra/admin: createConduit + channel management (deployer/operator-only). No normal-user signing path.

contracts.name: SeaDrop (primary NFT mint)
contracts.reason: DEFER (measured, user-facing but low/no usage — tracked in evidence Scope Classification): separate primary-mint product (mintPublic etc.), distinct decode target from Seaport order settlement, lower permission-risk (pays for new tokens, no order-signing of existing assets). Own surface to scope/measure separately.
